description: This figure presents experimental and simulated microstructure evolutions in low-alloy steel (0.398C-0.87Mn-0.31Si) under constant versus increasing temperature conditions at 0.01 s⁻¹ strain rate, comparing hot compression at constant 1050°C against a two-stage process increasing from 950°C to 1050°C during strain range 0.4–0.5. Panel (a) displays reconstructed EBSD inverse pole figure maps and corresponding GOS (Grain Orientation Spread) maps at 0.5 true strain, showing average grain sizes of 80.01±4.30 μm (DRX fraction 98.6%) for constant temperature versus 62.63±4.11 μm (DRX fraction 97.3%) for the increasing temperature path. Panel (b) provides reconstructed microstructures at 0.9 strain, revealing 81.30±4.39 μm for constant temperature versus 79.69±4.78 μm for increasing temperature. Panels (c) and (d) present 3D cellular automaton simulations: (c) shows simulated microstructures at constant 1050°C with grain sizes of 79.79 μm (0.5 strain, 98.2% DRX) and 79.89 μm (0.9 strain, 98.6% DRX), while (d) displays simulated multi-stage microstructures with color-coded grain origins (green: first stage at 950°C; red: second stage at 1050°C), showing grain sizes of 65.61 μm (0.5 strain, 99.5% DRX) and 75.90 μm (0.9 strain, 99.8% DRX). The figure demonstrates that the increasing temperature path yields finer grain structures compared to constant temperature processing, and validates that the inhomogeneous-dislocation-density 3D CA model accurately captures multi-stage dynamic recrystallization kinetics and grain size evolution under varying thermal conditions.
